expectations.text: Uses three-tier scope (MUST HAVE / SHOULD HAVE / OUT OF SCOPE)
expectations.evidence: Has explicit 'MUST HAVE', 'SHOULD HAVE', and 'EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE' sections with detailed feature lists

expectations.text: Creates GDD.md separate from CLAUDE.md (two distinct documents)
expectations.evidence: '1. GDD.md — A one-pager game design document' and '2. CLAUDE.md — The technical architecture document' listed as separate outputs

expectations.text: Mentions design pillars or core loop concept
expectations.evidence: 'Core loop: Explore -> Fight -> Survive -> Explore' and 'Elevator pitch and design pillars' both mentioned in GDD output spec

expectations.text: Mentions delegating to subagents for heavy work
expectations.evidence: No mention of subagents. Says 'I will use Godot MCP to create scenes' as if doing everything directly.

expectations.text: Specifies incremental build order with testing after each entity
expectations.evidence: 'We build incrementally: Player first, then slime, then combat, then world, then HUD. Each step gets tested before moving on.'

expectations.text: Includes anti-patterns from real sessions (body collision, area_entered, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Detailed 'Key Technical Decisions' table with 6 battle-tested patterns including Area2D, dual damage detection, cycle-based animation, CanvasLayer UI, pause during dialog, stun vs invincibility

expectations.text: Asks user to name the game
expectations.evidence: 'Game name -- Even a working title helps. Something like Slime Fields, Blade of the Wilds...'

expectations.text: Shows collision layers with explicit bitmask values (not just layer numbers)
expectations.evidence: Mentions 'Collision layer assignments (Player=2, Enemies=3, PlayerHitbox=4, EnemyHitbox=5)' but these are layer numbers, not bitmask values. Layer 3 has bitmask 4, not 3. Does not show both.

expectations.text: Mentions analyzing sprite sheets before writing animation code
expectations.evidence: 'I need to analyze the exact sheets before writing animation code' and asks about specific CraftPix packs
